old_claim.note: Technical Drawing Standards is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim_absent: No direct technical_drawing_standards edge remains because the corrected node is anchored to Teatro Olimpico's 1585 perspective stage, while the local technical_drawing_standards node is anchored to 1588 machine-book conventions.

source_shape.source_locator: Smarthistory dates Teatro Olimpico to 1580-1585 and identifies its forced-perspective stage set without tying the 1585 construction to later 1588 technical drawing standards.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports a 1585 Renaissance perspective-stage anchor independent of the later local technical-drawing node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping the edge would route a 1585 stage system through a 1588 prerequisite, so the old weak context edge fails chronology and direct-dependency tests.

ontology_before: Mechanical Theater Machinery depended on technical_drawing_standards as weak context, creating a later prerequisite once the node is anchored to 1585.
ontology_after: Renaissance Perspective Stage Machinery is anchored directly to theater-stage lineage and perspective art, without a direct technical_drawing_standards prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: technical_drawing_standards is absent as a direct prerequisite for mechanical_theater_machinery.
would_reject_if: The node were redated to a post-1588 machine-book manual scope such as Sabbattini's 1638 Pratica.
A source showed the 1585 Teatro Olimpico perspective stage depended on the specific technical_drawing_standards node.
The local technical_drawing_standards node were redated earlier and rescoped to include Teatro Olimpico design drawings.
short_reason: A 1588 technical-drawing node cannot be a direct prerequisite for a 1585 stage-system anchor.
